title: Candidate portal resume upload contained active API key and short-lived service token
preview:
Issue summary: A resume upload submitted through a third-party candidate portal contained a plaintext Redwood API key and an ephemeral service token in the resume text. Initial detection occurred via automated DLP patterns that flag high-entropy strings in uploads. Impact: potential exposure of a scoped API key and short-lived token associated with a non-privileged service account used by a CI job. Immediate concern is unauthorized access to internal test harness endpoints and the possibility of lateral token exchange.

Initial detection: 2026-03-09 08:14 UTC by DLP pipeline (upload-scan). The file was quarantined by the vendor but a copy was forwarded to our S3 for analysis (retention policy triggered).

Environment: Candidate portal operated by vendor TalentFlow (third-party SaaS). Upload landed in vendor-managed bucket and vendor forwarded a quarantine copy into our intake bucket in prod/us-east for manual review. The exposed API key appears to be a scoped key used by an internal integration (label: ci-integration-readonly) and the token appears to be a 1-hour service token created via internal token-exchange flow for onboarding automation.

High-level risk: Medium-High until key/token are rotated and audit completed. Potential for ephemeral access to internal test endpoints and leakage to unknown external actors if the file was downloaded before quarantine.

title: Ephemeral CI runner initContainer crashes due to Vault token refresh race during secret rewrap
preview:
Issue summary: Several ephemeral CI runners failed to finish job startup during the secrets rewrap event performed by Secrets Team (2026-03-10 01:13 UTC). The runner initContainer that fetches repo credentials from Vault exits with a 401/permission denied error and the pod moves to CrashLoopBackOff.
Impact: A subset (~8%) of ephemeral runners in prod-us-west failed to complete job bootstrapping for ~45 minutes, blocking CI jobs across multiple repos for internal teams. No user-facing product outages, but CI capacity degraded and release pipelines were delayed.
Environment: prod cluster (us-west), runner autoscaling group: ephemeral-runner-*-pool, Vault path: secret/ci/repos/*, Vault cluster: vault-prod-02.
Observed pattern: Failures correlated with a scheduled Vault rewrap (transit rekey) that rotated wrapping keys and reissued some service tokens. Affected pods show initContainer failing immediately when attempting to exchange the mounted token for Vault session credentials. A manual pod restart usually recovers the runner.

title: Vendor payment on hold: invoice currency / bank instruction mismatch for EU consulting vendor
preview: Issue summary: AP placed invoice NL-2026-1587 on hold. Invoice currency is EUR but vendor provided USD bank instructions. Finance Ops requires correct EUR IBAN/SEPA details and confirmation whether vendor expects NET30 from invoice date or payment on receipt. Impact: vendor is requesting payment escalation due to quarter close; potential relationship escalation if not resolved by SLA. Environment: production payments for EU vendors; payment run scheduled 2026-03-12.

title: Ambiguous legal-entity on consolidated vendor invoice causing tax allocation hold
preview:
Issue summary: A consolidated invoice (Vendor: GlobalCloud Services) was uploaded to the AP pipeline on 2026-03-02. The PDF contains charge lines attributable to two Redwood legal entities (Redwood Inference, Inc. - US and Redwood Inference Ltd. - UK) but the supplier did not provide entity-level purchase order (PO) mapping or VAT breakdown. The invoice was flagged by tax-validation as 'ambiguous legal entity' and moved to a manual hold queue.

Impact: Payment for the invoice (approx. $142,300 USD / £84,600 GBP equivalent) is blocked for both legal entities. Vendor has emailed requesting confirmation of payment date. This affects month-end close for the UK entity and may induce late-payment fees. Finance needs a deterministic allocation before releases to avoid misposting to tax and GL accounts.

Background: Vendor regularly issues consolidated monthlies that include UK professional services (GBP) and US cloud credits (USD). Historically the vendor provided a split invoice, but this month they consolidated without indicating which line items belong to each registered entity. AP automation dropped the invoice into manual review due to missing VAT/GL attributes and mismatched supplier legal names on the header vs. line items.

title: Create Slack <-> Confluence bridge: topic-tagging and automatic message translation for cross-region channels
preview:
Issue summary:
We need a lightweight Slack <-> Confluence bridge that adds structured topic tags to messages in selected cross-region channels and performs on-the-fly translation into the channel participants' preferred languages. This is to improve discoverability of operational discussions and reduce friction for non-EN teams.

Impact:
- Engineers in EU/APAC report slower context handoffs when incidents span regions.
- Important decisions and short runbook fragments posted in Slack are not consistently captured or tagged in Confluence.
- Manual translation or repeated explanation adds latency during incident response.

Goal / Request:
- Implement a bot/service that can: (1) suggest and apply a standardized topic tag (e.g., #deploy, #incident, #billing) to messages based on NLP classification, (2) post a short, tagged excerpt to a configured Confluence space/page (with linkback), and (3) optionally translate the message into a set of configured languages (start with en, fr, de, ja) when the channel has members with those locale preferences.
- Provide an admin UI or slash-commands to opt channels in/out and configure the Confluence mapping.
- The feature should respect DLP: allow opt-out per-channel and avoid sending messages from channels marked "sensitive" to Confluence or third-party translation APIs.

title: Provision 'Model Safety Review' pod: Confluence spaces, delegated admin groups, and timezone-aware ephemeral Slack standup channels
preview:
Issue summary:
We need a standardized project bootstrap for the new cross-functional 'Model Safety Review' pods. The request covers creating scoped Confluence spaces with templated pages, provisioning delegated Confluence admin groups per pod, and creating a set of ephemeral Slack channels for async/standup coordination with timezone-aware naming and archival automation.

Background and rationale:
- Several new pods will start working on safety evaluations and need consistent docs and low-friction collaboration spaces.
- Current ad-hoc creation leads to inconsistent permissions, missing watchers, and manual cleanup.
- Pods operate across APAC/EMEA/NA timezones; standup channels should be grouped by timezone to reduce noise.

Requested deliverables (high level):
1) Create a Confluence parent space named "Model Safety Review - <pod-name>" for each new pod (template to be applied). The space should have the following: a pre-populated "Evaluation Runbook" page, an "Artifacts & Results" page, and a "Decision Log" page.
2) Provision Confluence groups per pod: safety-<pod-name>-admins (Confluence admin role scoped to the space), safety-<pod-name>-viewers. Add initial users (list below) to appropriate groups.
3) Create Slack channels for each pod using naming pattern: #msr-<pod-name>-standup-<tz> (where <tz> is na / eme / apac). Channels must be created as private channels and have pinned templates for standup prompts.
4) Add automation: a) Add watchers (safety-<pod-name>-viewers) to the Confluence space; b) Configure a scheduled archival policy: Slack channels auto-archive after 90 days of inactivity; Confluence space set to be flagged for review after 180 days with a one-click archive button for platform ops.
5) Wire a simple Slack-to-Confluence bridge for standup summaries: a daily summary message posted to the space's "Daily Standups" page via the existing integration bot.

Initial pod list and membership to provision now:
- pod-name: firebreak-eval -> admins: [Marcus Kim, Lena Ortiz] -> viewers: [Anita Shah, Priya Rao, Carlos Mendes]
- pod-name: redwood-adversarial -> admins: [Jordan Blake] -> viewers: [Marcus Kim, Elena Park]

Acceptance criteria (see separate field for clarity): space templates exist, groups created and scoped, channels created with correct naming/visibility, automations configured and tested, owners notified.

title: Org policy deny on aws:TagCondition blocks cross-account ML deploys and GCP service-account impersonation
preview: Summary: Multiple ML deployment jobs for Dedicated customers failed when the provisioning pipeline attempted to assume a cross-account role and then impersonate a GCP service account. Impact: 6 deployment jobs for two enterprise tenants failed intermittently between 2026-03-01 02:10 UTC and 2026-03-01 03:45 UTC. Cause hypothesis: an AWS Organization Service Control Policy (SCP) with TagCondition-based denies blocks the sts:TagSession or ec2:RunInstances call used by the provisioner, causing the assume-role chain to fail and the downstream GCP impersonation to abort. This prevents creation of ephemeral credentials and blocks the dedicated capacity provisioning flow. User-visible impact: deployment retries surface 403/AccessDenied errors in the console and CI pipelines report "permission denied". Business impact: Dedicated customers in enterprise tier experienced delayed rollouts; no data exposure detected.

title: Provisional access hold & asset retention for 30-day temp rehire pending probation review
preview:
Issue summary:
HR has requested a temporary rehire for a former engineer (internal candidate) under a 30-day probation. HR needs a controlled, time-bound access posture: the employee should have only minimal service accounts and read-only console access during the probation window, and their previously issued company laptop must be retained in a secure hold state (not returned to circulation) until the probation completes and audit clearance is confirmed.

Impact:
- Candidate cannot start work until access posture is confirmed (delays go-live by an estimated 3 business days).
- Risk of overprivilege if previous access is reinstated without remediation (previous offboarding was performed but there are known lingering service accounts).

Background:
- Employee: former full-time SWE, rehired as contractor for 30 days pending probation review.
- Offboarding completed 2025-11-12; assets inventoried and laptop returned to inventory record #INV-2025-11-12-319.
- HR request asks to place the laptop on hold (don’t reassign) and to re-enable a minimal set of accounts only after manual approval.

Requested actions (HR):
1) Re-enable corporate email and SSO identity but keep membership in all privileged groups removed.
2) Recreate a scoped service account for CI read-only triggers only (no write permissions).
3) Place laptop INV-2025-11-12-319 into an "Evidence/Hold" tag in asset-management and lock assignment.
4) Do not reissue sudo or elevated IAM roles; block VPN until MFA re-enrollment and manager approval.
5) Schedule an access review at day 14 and day 28 of the probation window.

Constraints and policy questions:
- Does Redwood policy permit holding hardware out of circulation for a probationary rehiring period longer than the standard 14-day quarantine window? HR prefers 30 days to allow audit and manager sign-off.
- If we retain the old laptop on hold but re-enable SSO, do we need to rekey associated KMS/HSM-managed secrets or rotate service-account credentials to avoid potential continuity risks from previous deprovisioning?

Acceptance criteria:
- Identity re-enabled with explicit list of allowed groups and audit events are being emitted to the security log.
- Service account created with explicit deny-write scope and PR created documenting permissions.
- Asset-management entry marked "hold-for-probation" and locked from reassignment.
- Access review reminders scheduled and linked to this ticket.

Next steps requested from assignee:
- Confirm feasibility and provide estimated time to complete each action.
- Call out any policy blockers (KMS rotation, asset-hold duration) and recommended exception process if needed.

title: NVMe disk signature reorder after depot repair causes Intune/assetdb mismatch and Autopilot hostname collision
preview: Issue summary: After depot repair (NVMe replacement or reflow) some devices boot with the OS disk signature and partition GUIDs reordered. This triggers our assetdb/device fingerprinting logic to report a different Device ID back to Intune/Autopilot. Impact: devices appear as duplicate assets, Autopilot hostnames collide (hostname-1, hostname-1-1), and deployment policies targeted by device ID do not apply. Scope observed: ~7 devices from the same repair batch over the last two weeks. Not observed in fresh builds from imaging lab.

title: Oncall users missing access to new Grafana dashboards for rollout auto-rollback (SLO integration)
preview:
Internal oncall responders (SRE + service owners) are receiving links to the new rollout auto-rollback dashboards from incident-bot and the rollout controller audit timeline, but some users see "You do not have permission to view this dashboard" in Grafana.

This blocks oncall triage for the Auto-rollback on SLO breach integration rollout (especially during game day / staging-to-prod expansion). Request: update access groups/permissions so oncall rotations can view the dashboards by default, and document the process so future dashboard additions don’t repeat this.

Dashboards impacted (from observability-pack PR-913):
- "Rollouts: Auto-rollback events"
- "Rollouts: SLO evaluation outcomes"
- "Rollouts: Monitor staleness / missing data"

Who is impacted:
- #eng-oncall and #eng-sre responders
- Service owners paged on rollback events (non-SRE) who are in standard oncall access groups

Impact:
- Increased time-to-diagnosis during auto-rollback events
- Oncall cannot validate whether rollback trigger was legitimate vs monitor stale / suppression in effect

Requested outcome:
1) Add dashboards to the correct Grafana folder with oncall-viewer access.
2) Ensure "Oncall" access groups include permissions for the folder + datasource used by these dashboards.
3) Add short documentation snippet to the oncall readiness checklist / runbook explaining where access is granted and who owns it.

title: Prod audit-log extraction request for compliance review of customer data-handling
preview:
Issue summary: Compliance has requested a scoped extraction of production audit logs and inference metadata to validate BrightLeaf Health's data handling and redaction procedures.

Impact: The extraction must include request/response metadata, inference latency and model-version traces. Raw prompt/response text may contain PHI/PII; we must minimize exposure and follow approved handling controls.

Environment: prod / us-east (hosted Redwood runtime).

Requested window: 2026-02-01 through 2026-02-18 (inclusive).

Requested fields: timestamp, request_id, customer_id (hashed), model_version, token_count, latency_ms, status_code, response_hash. Compliance is requesting a separate mapping file of request_id -> original_customer_id accessible only to Legal. No raw prompt/response text unless explicitly approved after redaction.

Purpose: Third-party compliance review tied to contract clause 4.3. Compliance team (Sophie) needs the extract to demonstrate retention and redaction practices in an audit.

Access model proposed by Compliance: ephemeral extract job run by SRE in a sandboxed account, output encrypted and uploaded to pre-approved SFTP location. Access logged and reviewed.

Approvals required: Security (must confirm extraction script and redaction), Legal (confirm contractual scope), SRE (perform extraction and attest to access controls).

Deliverables requested: encrypted CSV of allowed fields, checksum manifest, extraction runbook, and attestations from SRE/Security/Legal.

title: Add RBAC permissions for rollout auto-rollback suppression/override (admin-only)
preview:
Requester: Felix Schneider (Console views / auditability)

Context
As part of the Auto-rollback on SLO breach integration, we are adding:
- Admin-only suppression windows ("do not auto-rollback for X")
- Admin-only manual override actions (pause/rollback) when auto-actions are enabled

We need RBAC updates so these controls are not available to general Console admins or service owners by default, and all usage is auditable.

Current behavior
- In staging, suppression/override endpoints are behind a feature flag but currently rely on a coarse "console_admin" check.
- This is too broad for production; Security requested explicit permissions and audit trail requirements before enabling for production services.

Request
1) Create/define new RBAC permissions (names below are suggested; fine to adjust to match existing RBAC conventions):
   - rollouts:auto_rollback:suppress (create/update/delete suppression window)
   - rollouts:auto_rollback:override (trigger manual pause/rollback action)
   - rollouts:auto_rollback:read_audit (view suppression/override decision details; links to monitor IDs/metrics snapshots)

2) Define default roles / groups:
   - Only members of "Rollouts Admin" (or equivalent) can suppress or override.
   - "SRE Oncall" can override during incident response, but suppression should require Rollouts Admin + justification.
   - Service owners should NOT get suppress/override by default.

3) Approval + audit requirements
   - Any suppression/override must require a free-text justification (min 20 chars) and should be recorded as an audit event with:
     - actor (user + auth principal)
     - permission used
     - target scope (rollout_id and/or service/route/model/region)
     - reason/justification
     - timestamp + request_id/correlation_id
   - Audit events must be retained per existing auto-action audit trail schema.

Acceptance criteria
- Console UI buttons for suppression/override are hidden/disabled unless permission is present.
- API returns 403 with clear error message when permission is missing.
- Audit logging is emitted for successful actions (and ideally for denied attempts with actor + target).
- Security sign-off from Kevin Osei before prod enablement.

Notes
This is a gating item for enabling suppression/override in production as part of the staged enablement plan. Please coordinate changes with the Console backend + policy/control plane owners so permission names are consistent across layers.

   - rollouts:auto_rollback:read_audit (view suppression/override decision details; links to monitor IDs/metrics snapshots)

2) Define default roles / groups:
   - Only members of "Rollouts Admin" (or equivalent) can suppress or override.
   - "SRE Oncall" can override during incident response, but suppression should require Rollouts Admin + justification.
   - Service owners should NOT get suppress/override by default.

   - Any suppression/override must require a free-text justification (min 20 chars) and should be recorded as an audit event with:

- Console UI buttons for suppression/override are hidden/disabled unless permission is present.

This is a gating item for enabling suppression/override in production as part of the staged enablement plan. Please coordinate changes with the Console backend + policy/control plane owners so permission names are consistent across layers.

title: Audit log pipeline alerts too noisy after rollout (drop-rate/latency) — tune thresholds + update runbook guidance
preview:
Issue Summary
- Since enabling the initial audit log delivery alerts (latency + drop rate), On-Call has been paged repeatedly for short-lived, low-impact fluctuations. The alerts are generating noise and are not aligned with the intended incident thresholds for audit pipeline reliability.

Impact
- Increased on-call load and alert fatigue for #eng-oncall / #eng-sre.
- Several pages triggered during expected/benign conditions (brief exporter backpressure, transient ingestion backlog recoveries, low-volume tenants).
- Risk: real incidents may get ignored or delayed due to repeated false positives.

What we’re seeing
- Drop-rate alert firing during short ingestion backlogs or during exporter retries, even when events are later delivered (at-least-once semantics).
- Latency alert firing on p95 spikes during low traffic periods, especially in regions with fewer events.
- Alerts firing per-dimension (region/service) without sufficient volume gating.

Scope
- Tune thresholds and evaluation windows for audit log pipeline alerts.
- Add volume gating / minimum sample requirements.
- Update the audit pipeline incident runbook to reflect new alert semantics and include a ‘noise triage’ checklist.

Notes / Context
- The audit log delivery SLO and dashboards were recently rolled out; the current alerting configuration appears overly sensitive.
- This ticket is intended to adjust alerting behavior without changing core pipeline delivery semantics.

Proposed changes (implementation guidance)
1) Drop-rate alert
- Change evaluation from short-window percentage to burn-rate style alerting with multiple windows (fast + slow) to reduce flapping.
- Add minimum-event-volume gating (do not page if below N events in window).
- Keep a lower-severity warning notification for early signals (non-paging).

2) Latency alert
- Page only on sustained p95 delivery latency above threshold for >= 15m (instead of 5m), plus volume gating.
- Prefer region-aggregated SLO for paging; keep per-region/per-service as non-paging signals.

3) Exporter queue/backpressure
- Convert “queue depth” alerts to warnings unless backlog grows monotonically for >= 20m.
- Ensure dashboards show “ingestion backlog vs exporter backlog” to route the incident correctly.

Acceptance Criteria
- Paging alerts align with actionable incidents (sustained drop-rate or sustained high delivery latency).
- On-call pages reduce materially (target: <2 pages/week for audit pipeline absent real incident).
- Runbook updated with new thresholds, triage steps, and links to the relevant dashboards.

Owner / Reviewers
- Owner: Sean Gallagher (SRE)
- Consult: Rafael Mendes (runbook), Aisha Bello (alerting/dashboards), Logan Wright (coverage telemetry impacts)

title: Request access for Security + GRC to Audit Log Coverage Dashboard (least-privilege roles)
preview:
Requesting access for Security and GRC to the new “Audit Log Coverage (Sensitive Actions)” dashboard used for evidence collection and ongoing monitoring.

Business need
- SOC 2 / ISO-aligned evidence: demonstrate monitoring + coverage tracking for sensitive actions audit events.

title: Request: add/assign 'Quality Gate Override Approver' role for Optimize time-bound overrides (Console RBAC)
preview:
Request type: Access / RBAC change

Background:
Optimize Quality Gates are now blocking Apply when a gate fails. We have a time-bound override flow (admin-controlled) for documented exceptions (see exception policy). During limited production rollout, we need a designated oncall/PM to be able to approve overrides in Console without using a shared admin account.

What is needed:
1) Create (or confirm) a dedicated RBAC role/group for this capability:
   - Role name (proposal): quality_gate_override_approver
   - Group name (proposal): optimize-quality-gate-override-approvers
2) Permissions required (least privilege):
   - Approve/deny time-bound override requests for Optimize config changes (batching/caching/quant profile)
   - View gate run details + artifacts links sufficient to make decision
   - View audit log entries related to overrides (read-only)
   - No ability to edit gating thresholds, disable gating globally, or directly apply configs outside the approved override workflow
3) Scope:
   - Hosted API (prod) routes/accounts participating in limited rollout
   - Console UI + underlying approval endpoint

Requested users to be added (initial set):
- Paula Mendes (PM, Rollouts/telemetry) — backup approver for business-hours incidents
- Sean Gallagher (SRE lead) — oncall escalation approver for after-hours/high-severity customer impact

Constraints / policy alignment:
- Overrides must be time-bound and require reason + ticket link per policy
- Approver actions must be captured in audit logs (who/when/what/expiration)
- Prefer SSO group mapping if available; otherwise Console-native group assignment is acceptable for beta

Why now:
We had a near-miss where an optimization change needed rapid override to unblock a customer, but the only available path was a full admin role (too broad). We want an audited, limited approver role before expanding rollout.

Acceptance criteria:
- Named users can approve/deny override requests in Console
- Actions write audit events visible in audit log export
- Users cannot perform broader admin actions (validated via negative test)
- Security sign-off on scope/permissions

Notes:
If feasible, also include ability for Release Engineering oncall to temporarily be added during incident response with a 24h expiration on membership.

title: Activation funnel dashboard discrepancy (EU-West drop + mismatch vs warehouse) after lifecycle growth instrumentation changes
preview:
Issue summary
- Growth/Marketing and Product flagged a sudden drop and cross-source mismatch in the activation funnel metrics (signup → API key created → first request) used for the Lifecycle Growth Experiments sprint.
- Primary symptom: EU-West conversion from signup → api_key_created appeared ~12–18% lower than US-East starting 2026-01-21 18:00 UTC, and overall “new signups” appeared higher than expected vs auth/user tables.
- Impact: Week 1–2 experiment readouts risked being invalid/noisy; we paused one planned expansion of the email A/B until numbers were reconciled.

Impact
- Affected dashboards: Activation Funnel (Observability Pack) + internal Looker view used by Growth.
- Impacted decisions: stop/go thresholds and lift calculations for onboarding email v2 and console nudge experiments.
- Customer impact: none direct (reporting/measurement issue only), but could cause us to make incorrect product/GT-M decisions.

Detection
- Reported via Slack #help thread ("events missing in one region") and follow-up in #eng-platform on 2026-01-22.
- Observability Pack funnel panel showed EU-West drop; Mei Lin noted mismatch vs daily new users in warehouse.

What changed recently
- PR-28431: added/standardized activation events for signup → api key → first request → first 100k tokens.
- PR-241: new dashboard panels + funnel query definitions.
- Console nudge framework shipped behind flags; additional event properties and cohort assignment introduced during the week.

Initial hypotheses
1) Ingestion lag or partial pipeline outage in EU-West.
2) Region routing misconfiguration (EU events landing in US dataset or vice versa).
3) Event validation rejecting records due to missing required properties (user_id, event_id) for EU only.
4) Duplicate event emission inflating baseline in one region, making EU look like it dropped.

Investigation notes (high level)
- Confirmed ingestion health: Kafka consumer lag normal; no sustained drop in raw event volume by region at the edge collectors.
- Cross-checked three sources for the same window (2026-01-21 00:00–2026-01-22 23:59 UTC):
  - Raw collector logs: stable event counts in eu-west.
  - Warehouse fact_events table: stable api_key_created events in eu-west.
  - Activation Funnel dashboard: apparent eu-west drop.
- Found the discrepancy was query-side + dedupe behavior:
  - The funnel panel’s Step 1 “signup” was keyed off signup_created events and deduped by (user_id, day), but the Step 1 event was being emitted twice for a subset of flows in US-East due to a console/auth edge case.
  - EU-West had fewer duplicates, so the “conversion rate” (step2/step1) looked artificially worse in EU-West when comparing regions.
  - Additionally, the dashboard query used a join that unintentionally filtered some EU users when event_time skew occurred near midnight UTC (session boundary + region bucket), amplifying the appearance of a regional issue.

Root cause
- Duplicate emission of signup_created/signup_completed for users who hit both (a) email verification completion and (b) immediate console onboarding flow that retriggered the signup event. This was more common in US-East due to traffic mix and a feature-flag exposure path.
- Dashboard query logic for Step 1 + Step 2 used inconsistent dedupe keys (Step 1 deduped by user_id/day; Step 2 deduped by event_id) and a region/date join condition that excluded a portion of EU events at boundary times.
- Net effect: inflated Step 1 counts in US-East and undercounted Step 2 in EU-West in the visualization layer, while the warehouse raw events were largely correct.

Resolution
- Engineering fix: PR-28521 shipped to stop duplicate signup event emission; verified by before/after comparison on a canary cohort and then full rollout.
- Analytics fix: updated Activation Funnel panel query to use consistent dedupe (event_id preferred, fallback to (user_id, event_name, request_id) for older records) and removed the join condition that filtered EU events at UTC boundaries.
- Backfill: no backfill required for canonical fact tables; dashboard now recomputes accurately from warehouse. Noted that historical conversion rates for 2026-01-19 to 2026-01-22 should be interpreted with caution; we annotated the dashboard.

Validation
- Post-fix checks (2026-01-26):
  - Regional conversion rates are within expected variance (eu-west no longer showing step-change).
  - New user counts align within ~1–2% between auth table and deduped signup events.
  - Funnel step drop-offs align with expected product behavior and match secondary metrics (API key creation table).

Follow-ups
- Add a data-quality alert: spike in duplicate rate for signup_created and api_key_created by region.
- Add unit tests/contract tests for event emission idempotency in console/auth flows.
- Document dedupe keys and “source of truth” definitions in the activation metrics notes + dashboard README.

Stakeholders
- Growth: Ben Carter (experiment owner), Tyler Benson (attribution)
- Product: Marissa Cole (console), Paula Mendes (telemetry/rollouts)
- Eng/Analytics: Logan Wright (assigned), Nadia Rahman, Mei Lin

References
- Slack: #help (tracking discrepancy), #eng-platform (taxonomy + dedupe)
- Related PRs: PR-28431, PR-28521, Observability Pack PR-241

title: Validate/adjust DKIM/SPF/DMARC + SendGrid authenticated domain config for scaling lifecycle email volume
preview:
Request
- We are ramping volume for the Hosted API onboarding email v2 as part of the Lifecycle Growth Experiments sprint.
- Need confirmation that Redwood’s sending domain authentication is correctly set up and aligned with our current provider (SendGrid), and that SPF/DKIM/DMARC are passing with alignment for the From domain.

Background / impact
- We saw an increase in “didn’t receive onboarding email” reports and deliverability variability as we started ramping (esp. Gmail).
- Risk: scaling volume without correct authentication will amplify spam folder placement and/or bounces, and invalidate experiment readouts.

Scope
1) DNS: verify SPF record(s) for the From domain used by lifecycle emails.
2) DNS: verify DKIM keys (CNAMEs) for SendGrid authenticated domain are present and matching SendGrid UI.
3) DNS: verify DMARC exists and is reasonable for our current maturity (monitor first, then tighten).
4) SendGrid: verify authenticated domain + custom return-path/bounce domain configured, and that the From address matches the authenticated domain.

What I need from Internal Support / Marketing Ops
- Confirm current “From” domain and SendGrid authenticated domain.
- Provide exact DNS records required (host/name + type + value) and confirm after propagation.
- Provide quick validation output (header authentication results) for a test message.

Notes
- We’ll keep ramp conservative until authentication is confirmed.
- If DMARC is missing or misconfigured, propose minimal safe config (p=none + rua) to start.

References
- Related customer ticket volume: SUP-24871 (Not receiving onboarding emails)
- Ongoing deliverability thread with vendor support referenced in sprint notes (SendGrid investigation)

Acceptance criteria
- DKIM: PASS for lifecycle emails with alignment to the From domain.
- SPF: PASS with alignment (or documented rationale if using subdomain/return-path alignment).
- DMARC: PASS (at least p=none) with reporting enabled.
- Document final records + where to monitor (SendGrid, Postmaster Tools).

title: Console: Rollback button not visible for on-call operator (RBAC permission denied / role mapping misconfigured)
preview:
Issue summary
- On-call operator cannot see the "Rollback" button in the Console for Optimization Config versions.
- This blocks rollback during gameday / incident response and is confusing because the operator is in the expected on-call group.

Impact
- Internal: on-call cannot execute one-click rollback via Console (must escalate to Platform team or use break-glass).
- Risk: delayed mitigation if a bad optimization config causes latency/5xx regression.

Who is affected
- Internal users in Okta/SSO group: sre-oncall (expected to have Optimization Change Operator privileges).

Observed behavior
- UI: No "Rollback" CTA shown on version detail page.
- API: direct call to rollback endpoint returns 403.

Expected behavior
- Members of the on-call operator group should have permission to rollback optimization configs within approved scopes.

Error details
- Console network call response:
  - POST /v1/optimization-configs/rollouts/{rollout_id}:rollback
  - HTTP 403
  - Body: {"error":"permission_denied","message":"missing permission: optimization_config.rollback"}

Notes
- This started after the RBAC policy bundle update earlier this week (Console safe-apply/rollback internal beta enablement).

- Sales Engineering team members cannot access the Redwood demo org in Console. Console shows either (a) no orgs available, or (b) org visible but all pages 403.

Impact:
- Blocks live demo prep and prevents SE from running the pre-demo checklist (healthcheck, dashboards verification, key rotation).
- Affects multiple SEs; AE access appears unaffected.

Who is affected:
- Markus Klein and 2 additional SEs (names redacted in ticket; can share via Slack DM if needed).

Expected:
- SEs should have access to the demo org with the standard “Demo SE” role (read/write routes + view usage/observability; no billing admin).

Actual:

2) Attempt to select org “Redwood Demo (GTM)”.
3) Observe: org not present OR selecting it leads to 403 on Projects/Routes.

Notes:
- This started after a demo-tenant reset earlier this week (suspected change in group→role mapping or org role template).

title: Grant least-privilege permissions for perf-canary service account (inference read + metrics write)
preview:
Request type: IAM / service account permissions

Requester: Noah Patel (Serving Runtime)
Service: perf-canary (prod)
Service account: svc-perf-canary@prod.redwood-inference.iam (created via terraform; see pr-18712)
Target start date: Week of 2026-01-19 (phase-1 rollout prep)

Background:
We’re rolling out the always-on perf-canary that sends synthetic requests to internal inference endpoints and emits per-model performance metrics (latency, tokens/sec, queueing breakdown). Canary does not use customer prompts; only synthetic prompt shapes.

Needed permissions (least privilege):
1) Inference read/invoke:
- Ability for svc-perf-canary to call internal inference endpoints in prod for a curated allowlist of models.
- Auth scope expected: "canary:invoke" (added in pr-19197). Please ensure the service account is bound to this scope in prod.
- Network path: via internal API gateway (same as other internal services). No console access needed.

2) Metrics write:
- Ability to emit metrics to telemetry ingestion (Prometheus remote_write / metrics gateway used by services).
- Expected permission/role: telemetry.metrics:write (or equivalent internal role used for service metrics emission).
- No log ingestion or trace write required for MVP (metrics only).

Non-goals / explicitly NOT requested:
- No ability to read customer prompts, request payload logs, or production data stores.
- No admin access to model registry, control plane, or runtime fleets.

Acceptance criteria:
- perf-canary can successfully call the internal inference endpoint in prod using svc-perf-canary credentials and receives 200 responses.
- perf-canary can emit metrics without 403/permission errors (confirmed by metrics appearing in Grafana/Prometheus within ~5–10 minutes).

Operational notes:
- Canary QPS is capped; overhead budget <0.5% GPU capacity per region.
- We will validate in a single region (us-east) with 2–3 models before enabling paging.

Point of contact for validation:
- Noah Patel (runtime perf canary TL)
- Nadia Rahman (telemetry pipeline)

Please advise if you need the exact endpoint hostname and/or metrics remote_write destination; we can provide in a secure channel.

title: Benchmark fleet service account missing IAM/RBAC permissions (GPU jobs + artifact export)
preview:
Issue summary
- The benchmark fleet runner service account cannot (a) create/run GPU benchmark jobs reliably and (b) export run artifacts (manifests, traces, JSON results) to the shared artifacts bucket.
- This started after the benchmark fleet moved to Workload Identity + a new namespace split for perf-canary vs ad-hoc runs.

Impact
- Blocks Q1 2026 perf baseline sweeps and perf-canary nightly runs from producing publishable artifacts.
- Engineers can sometimes run locally, but CI and scheduled runs fail, leading to missing data in dashboards and delayed regression triage.

Who is affected
- Serving Runtime benchmarking owners + SRE perf-canary pipeline.

Environment
- Benchmark fleet Kubernetes clusters: bench-us-east (primary), bench-eu-west (secondary)
- Namespace(s): benchmark-lab, perf-canary
- Runner identity:
  - KSA: perf-runner (namespace: perf-canary)
  - KSA: benchmark-runner (namespace: benchmark-lab)
  - GSA (expected): bench-fleet-runner@redwood-inference.iam.gserviceaccount.com
- Artifact destinations:
  - GCS: gs://rw-benchmark-artifacts-us-east1/perf-suite/
  - GCS: gs://rw-benchmark-artifacts-eu-west1/perf-suite/
  - (optional) BigQuery dataset used by dashboard ingest: rw_perf_bench

Symptoms / errors
1) GPU jobs fail at image pull (intermittent but common)
- Pod events:
  - Failed to pull image "us-docker.pkg.dev/redwood-prod/benchmark-lab/runner:2026.01.14": rpc error: code = Unknown desc = failed to authorize: failed to fetch anonymous token: unexpected status from GET request to https://us-docker.pkg.dev/v2/token: 403 Forbidden
  - ImagePullBackOff

2) Artifact export fails at the end of runs
- Export step logs:
  - gsutil cp results.json gs://rw-benchmark-artifacts-us-east1/perf-suite/INT-8821-test/...
  - AccessDeniedException: 403 bench-fleet-runner@redwood-inference.iam.gserviceaccount.com does not have storage.objects.create access to the Google Cloud Storage object.

3) Some runs create pods but fail to attach GPU resources in perf-canary namespace
- Kubernetes error surfaced by harness:
  - Error from server (Forbidden): pods is forbidden: User "system:serviceaccount:perf-canary:perf-runner" cannot create resource "pods" in API group "" in the namespace "perf-canary"

Steps to reproduce
1) From CI (perf-canary) trigger the representative suite nightly job.
2) Observe failure at either:
  - Pod creation in perf-canary namespace (RBAC Forbidden), or
  - Image pull from Artifact Registry (403), or
  - Result upload to GCS bucket (403).

Investigation notes (internal)
- Confirmed failures on both us-east and eu-west benchmark clusters.
- K8s service accounts exist but are not consistently bound to the expected Google service account.
- RBAC in perf-canary namespace diverged from benchmark-lab after namespace split; benchmark-lab had RoleBinding granting create/list/watch pods/jobs, perf-canary did not.
- Artifact Registry access appears to require explicit reader permission on the repo; previously inherited via a broader project-level role that was removed during least-privilege cleanup.
- GCS bucket permissions were granted to the old GSA (bench-fleet@...), not the new bench-fleet-runner@... account.

Root cause
A) Kubernetes RBAC missing in perf-canary namespace
- The perf-canary namespace was created without the Role/RoleBinding that grants the runner KSA permissions to create Jobs/Pods and read Pod status/events.

B) Workload Identity binding incomplete for one KSA
- The KSA (perf-canary/perf-runner) was not annotated with iam.gke.io/gcp-service-account=bench-fleet-runner@... in one cluster, causing requests to use the node default identity (no access).

C) IAM regressions during least-privilege cleanup
- bench-fleet-runner@... lacked:
  - Artifact Registry read access to the benchmark-lab runner repository
  - GCS object create permissions on rw-benchmark-artifacts-* buckets
  - Logging writer permissions for structured benchmark logs

Resolution
1) Fixed K8s RBAC (both clusters)
- Added Role + RoleBinding in perf-canary namespace to allow the runner to:
  - create/get/list/watch pods
  - create/get/list/watch batch/jobs
  - get/list/watch pods/log and events

2) Fixed Workload Identity binding
- Annotated KSAs:
  - perf-canary/perf-runner
  - benchmark-lab/benchmark-runner
  with the intended GSA bench-fleet-runner@redwood-inference.iam.gserviceaccount.com
- Added iam.workloadIdentityUser binding to allow those KSAs to impersonate the GSA.

3) Restored required GCP IAM roles to bench-fleet-runner@...
- Artifact Registry:
  - Granted roles/artifactregistry.reader on repo: redwood-prod/benchmark-lab
- GCS buckets:
  - Granted roles/storage.objectAdmin on:
    - rw-benchmark-artifacts-us-east1 (restricted to perf-suite/* prefix via bucket policy where supported)
    - rw-benchmark-artifacts-eu-west1 (restricted similarly)
- Observability:
  - Granted roles/logging.logWriter (to ensure harness logs are not dropped)
- Dashboard ingest (optional path used by nightly):
  - Granted minimal BigQuery dataset write role to rw_perf_bench ingest service (not to the runner) and updated pipeline to use ingest SA instead of runner SA (reduces blast radius).

Verification
- Ran a smoke benchmark in perf-canary namespace on us-east:
  - Pod scheduled with GPU, image pulled successfully, job completed.
  - Artifact upload succeeded; results.json + manifest.json present under gs://rw-benchmark-artifacts-us-east1/perf-suite/smoke/2026-01-16/
- Re-ran on eu-west:
  - Same outcome; no 403s.
- Confirmed perf-canary nightly run unblocked (next scheduled run expected 2026-01-16 03:00 UTC).

Follow-ups / notes
- Please keep IAM changes in Terraform (single source of truth). Manual console grants were avoided; any emergency grants done during triage were removed after Terraform apply.
- Recommend adding a startup check in the harness to assert:
  - can create Job in target namespace
  - can read/write to artifact bucket
  - can pull runner image
  and fail fast with actionable error.

Requester notes
- Rina: needs this resolved before the next baseline sweep window reservation (Q1 2026 baseline plan).

title: Office printer island - intermittent print queue failures and network timeouts
preview:
Issue summary: Several staff on floor 3 (printer island A/B/C) report intermittent failures when submitting print jobs. Jobs appear in the client queue but never reach the printer; sometimes jobs error with a network timeout or 'printer not found'.

Impact: Low-medium operational impact; employees are using floor printers for contracts and physical sign-offs. Reported across 6 users over the last 48 hours; not a full outage but recurring and disruptive.

Environment: On-prem office network, wired VLAN 12 (office-devices), printers are HP LaserJet managed via local print server (windows-printsvc-03). Office DHCP and printer IPs appear stable. Printers are assigned static reservations.

Steps to reproduce:
1. From a macOS or Windows client on VLAN 12, submit a 20-page print job to Printer-Island-A.
2. Observe that the job appears in the local client queue.
3. Wait 30-120s; some jobs remain queued and eventually error with timeout OR disappear from client queue without printing.
4. From the print server, check active spool and filter logs for job id.

Logs/observations:
- Windows print server (windows-printsvc-03) shows frequent 'spooler timeout' entries around 10:23–10:45 on 2025-11-11.
- Printer diagnostics show occasional small packet loss on management interface (snmp ping latency spikes to 300ms) but connectivity returns.
- No DNS changes or recent network maintenance recorded in the ticket history for that time window.
- Affected users are both wired and wireless on the same VLAN; guest VLAN unaffected.

Hypothesis: Intermittent network congestion or managed switch flaps on the office floor are causing SMB/LPD sessions to drop mid-spool. Alternatively, print spooler on windows-printsvc-03 may be hitting resource limits under specific job sizes.

Temporary workaround: Ask users to re-submit smaller batched jobs (split larger docs). If urgent, use the single-office color printer with direct USB queue (manual).

Next steps (proposed):
- Engineering: collect and attach spooler logs for 2025-11-11 through 2025-11-13 (Miguel).
- Networking: enable interface error counters and port-level logging for switch-shelf-3B for 72 hours (NetOps).
- Ops: attempt a controlled restart of windows-printsvc-03 spooler during low-hours (overnight maintenance window).

Related artifacts: link to temporary diagnostics doc: drive:shared/office-it/printer-diagnostics-2025-11 (placeholder).

title: Log retention exception  QuantaGov (extended retention for audit)
preview:
Issue summary:
QuantaGov (Private VPC deployment) requests longer retention for inference request logs for audit purposes.

Requested retention:
- Payload: 180 days
- Metadata: 365 days

Notes:
This is a Private deployment. Customer uses Redwood-managed telemetry exporter into their VPC-managed log store, but Redwood is still responsible for the default TTL config in the exporter.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Exporter config updated to 180d payload and 365d metadata for QuantaGov only.
2) Security approval documented.
3) Customer acceptance recorded (security questionnaire / email).

References:
- Policy: confluence://security-and-compliance/data-residency-and-retention/inference-request-log-retention-policy
- Exception register: confluence://security-and-compliance/risk-and-exceptions/inference-log-retention-exception-register

title: Private observability minimum pack: CI smoke tests failing (Prometheus scrape targets down / dashboards not detected)
preview:
Owner requested: SRE/Infra to triage CI instability in the Private Observability Minimum Pack smoke-test pipeline.

Issue summary
- Our GitHub Actions job "private-observability-smoke" is intermittently failing on main for redwood-private-installer + redwood-helm-charts integration.
- Failures started after the smoke-test harness changes landed and we began validating dashboard provisioning + Prometheus scrape targets as part of the gate.

Impact
- Blocks merges/releases touching the Private observability minimum pack because CI gate is red.
- Consumes SRE/Eng time due to retries; reduces confidence in pack validation.

When/where
- Pipeline: CI (GitHub Actions), kind cluster, ephemeral.
- Job: "smoke-test-private-observability" (step: post-install validation).
- First observed: 2025-08-18 evening; escalated 2025-08-19.

Symptoms (from CI output)
1) Prometheus targets never reach healthy state within timeout
- Validation expects all Redwood ServiceMonitors to be "up" within 180s.
- Error:
  - "FAIL: expected Prometheus target job=redwood-serving-runtime to be up; got state=down, lastError=context deadline exceeded"

2) Grafana dashboards provisioned but API query returns empty folder on first attempt
- Error:
  - "FAIL: expected >= 3 dashboards in folder 'Redwood Private - Minimum Pack'; got 0"

Suspected regression area
- Prometheus Operator version drift in CI base image / chart dependency.
- ServiceMonitor label selector mismatch between helm chart defaults and kube-prometheus-stack in CI.
- Race: Grafana sidecar loads dashboards async; our check hits Grafana too early.

How to reproduce locally
- On a dev machine with docker/kind:
  1. ./scripts/ci/smoke/private_observability_smoke.sh
  2. Or: make smoke-test-private-observability
- Expected: install completes, Prometheus scrapes Redwood targets, Grafana folder contains Redwood dashboards.
- Actual: intermittent failure ~40-60% across retries.

Logs (snippets)
- From CI run 2025-08-19T10:42Z:
  - "[validate] waiting for prometheus targets..."
  - "GET http://prometheus-k8s.monitoring:9090/api/v1/targets"
  - "target=serviceMonitor/redwood-private/serving-runtime state=down lastError='server returned HTTP status 403 Forbidden'"
  - "NOTE: NetworkPolicy in namespace monitoring blocks ingress from prometheus-k8s -> redwood-private"

- From CI run 2025-08-19T15:08Z:
  - "[grafana] GET /api/search?folderIds=23"
  - "HTTP 200 []"
  - "[grafana] sidecar log: 'processing 7 dashboard files' (appears ~45s after our first query)"

Attachments / references
- Slack thread: #internal-support (linked from SRE triage channel) referencing #eng-infra discussion about prom operator + selectors.
- Customer-relevant context: SUP-49210 (missing dashboards) and SUP-49238 (noisy alerts) were both triggered by packaging changes; we want CI to catch real regressions, not flake.

Request
- Infra/SRE to identify root cause and propose a stable fix (chart pin, selector alignment, timeout/backoff strategy, or policy adjustment).

Impact
- Blocks merges/releases touching the Private observability minimum pack because CI gate is red.
- Consumes SRE/Eng time due to retries; reduces confidence in pack validation.

When/where
- Pipeline: CI (GitHub Actions), kind cluster, ephemeral.
- Job: "smoke-test-private-observability" (step: post-install validation).
- First observed: 2025-08-18 evening; escalated 2025-08-19.

Symptoms (from CI output)
1) Prometheus targets never reach healthy state within timeout
- Validation expects all Redwood ServiceMonitors to be "up" within 180s.
- Error:
  - "FAIL: expected Prometheus target job=redwood-serving-runtime to be up; got state=down, lastError=context deadline exceeded"

2) Grafana dashboards provisioned but API query returns empty folder on first attempt
- Error:
  - "FAIL: expected >= 3 dashboards in folder 'Redwood Private - Minimum Pack'; got 0"

Suspected regression area
- Prometheus Operator version drift in CI base image / chart dependency.
- ServiceMonitor label selector mismatch between helm chart defaults and kube-prometheus-stack in CI.
- Race: Grafana sidecar loads dashboards async; our check hits Grafana too early.

How to reproduce locally
- On a dev machine with docker/kind:
  1. ./scripts/ci/smoke/private_observability_smoke.sh
  2. Or: make smoke-test-private-observability
- Expected: install completes, Prometheus scrapes Redwood targets, Grafana folder contains Redwood dashboards.
- Actual: intermittent failure ~40-60% across retries.

  - "target=serviceMonitor/redwood-private/serving-runtime state=down lastError='server returned HTTP status 403 Forbidden'"

  - "HTTP 200 []"

- Infra/SRE to identify root cause and propose a stable fix (chart pin, selector alignment, timeout/backoff strategy, or policy adjustment).

title: HubSpot API rate limits (HTTP 429) intermittently blocking PLG signal task creation
preview:
Issue summary
- Our PLG signal router is intermittently failing to create HubSpot tasks and timeline notes due to HubSpot API returning HTTP 429 (rate limit).
- Impact is missed or delayed follow-up tasks for the NA pilot cohort. Company property upserts are also occasionally skipped when task creation fails mid-batch.

Impact
- Missed tasks: ~15–40 tasks/day during peak (pilot week 1) based on router logs and expected routed_signals count.
- AEs are not seeing required follow-up tasks in their queues; routing appears "successful" for some signals because company property updates may succeed while task creation fails.
- Risk: reduced conversion + duplicate outreach (if backfilled later without dedupe).

Who is impacted
- Internal: Sales (NA pilot), Sales Ops, Marketing Ops
- Systems: signal-router service, HubSpot integration client

Environment
- prod (us-east)
- HubSpot app: "Redwood Signal Router" (OAuth)

Symptoms
- Spike in 429s from HubSpot across endpoints:
  - /crm/v3/objects/companies/* (property upsert)
  - /crm/v3/objects/tasks (task create)
  - /crm/v3/objects/notes (timeline note)
- Router retries are currently too aggressive and synchronized across workers, causing retry storms.

Steps to reproduce (observed)
1) Backfill or run daily aggregation that yields >N qualified signals in a short window.
2) Router processes signals concurrently (multiple workers).
3) HubSpot responds 429; router retries quickly with limited jitter.
4) Some tasks never created (max retries reached) and signals marked "routed" in internal analytics because the pipeline marks success after company update.

Evidence / logs
- Representative log lines from signal-router (redacted OAuth token and IDs):

2025-05-19T16:22:11.904Z  INFO hubspot.request endpoint=/crm/v3/objects/tasks method=POST request_id=hs_01HYC0G7... company_id=113492820 signal_id=plg_sig_01J2... 
2025-05-19T16:22:12.118Z  WARN hubspot.response status=429 retry_after=10s body={"status":"error","message":"You have reached your limit. Please try again in a few seconds.","correlationId":"b5e2c6a0-..."}
2025-05-19T16:22:12.119Z  WARN hubspot.retry attempt=1 backoff_ms=500
2025-05-19T16:22:12.643Z  WARN hubspot.response status=429 retry_after=10s correlation_id=64a8c9f7-...
2025-05-19T16:22:12.644Z ERROR hubspot.task_create_failed attempts=5 final_status=429 signal_id=plg_sig_01J2... company_id=113492820

- Similar failures observed on company update:

2025-05-19T16:20:04.003Z  INFO hubspot.request endpoint=/crm/v3/objects/companies/113492820 method=PATCH request_id=hs_01HYC0F...
2025-05-19T16:20:04.201Z  WARN hubspot.response status=429 retry_after=10s correlation_id=0f3d1c2b-...

Expected behavior
- Router should respect HubSpot rate limits and Retry-After headers, apply exponential backoff with jitter, and queue/replay safely so tasks are eventually created.
- Router should not mark a signal as fully routed until both note + task creation succeed (or is explicitly suppressed / moved to dead-letter with alerting).

Current behavior
- Router hits rate limit during bursts and drops some task/note creations after exhausting retries.

Notes
- Slack thread with parallel mitigation discussion: #eng-platform "hubspot-429s-seen-in-prod-mitigations" (ref: 1747678900)
- NA pilot sensitivity: avoid duplicate tasks if we re-run backfills; need idempotency key strategy for task creation where possible.

Requested outcome
- Implement robust rate-limit handling (Retry-After, per-endpoint throttling, queueing) and ensure idempotent routing.
- Add metrics/alerts so Ops sees 429 spikes and dropped operations.

title: Burst pool scale failures: initially triaged as runtime memory pressure; confirmed quota exhaustion
preview:
Issue summary:
Hosted API burst pool failed to scale during traffic spike. Runtime reported allocation failures that looked like OOM, but node provisioning did not happen (quota ceiling).

Impact:
Intermittent 429s and latency spikes for burst routes for ~25m.

Triage notes:
- Hypothesis A: runtime memory fragmentation/OOM
- Hypothesis B: scheduler placement bug
- Confirmed: provisioning blocked by GPU quota ceiling; runtime errors were a downstream symptom

Resolution:
- Quota increased and burst pool scale policy adjusted
- Added dashboard panel for quota headroom vs pending nodes

title: Enable Dedicated staged rollouts feature flag for beta design partners (account/pool scoped)
preview:
Request: enable the Dedicated staged rollouts beta feature flag for specific Dedicated accounts/pools so CS/TAM + SRE can run staged upgrades with gate evaluation and rollback semantics.

Context:
- Project: "Dedicated staged rollouts for reserved pools"
- We are moving from internal dogfood to limited beta (1–2 design partners) and need scoped enablement by account + pool (+ region where applicable).
- This is NOT general customer self-serve; only for beta accounts with operator involvement.

Requested enablement scope (prod only):
1) Customer: Lattice AI (Dedicated)
   - Account ID: acct_9f2d3c1b
   - Dedicated pool(s):
     - pool_rsrv_7c1b2a (primary) regions: us-east-1, us-west-2
   - Console org slug: lattice-ai

2) Customer: EmberSearch (Dedicated)
   - Account ID: acct_21a6b0fe
   - Dedicated pool(s):
     - pool_rsrv_5a88e1 regions: us-east-1 only
   - Console org slug: embersearch

Feature flags / toggles:
- Flag name (control plane): ff.dedicated_rollouts.staged.enabled
- Flag name (console): ff.console.dedicated_rollouts.staged_ui
- Optional (policy engine automated gates): ff.dedicated_rollouts.gates_automated_v1 (enable only if SRE approves; otherwise manual gate mode)

Desired behavior after enablement:
- Console shows "Staged rollouts" workflow for the above pools only.
- Control plane accepts DedicatedPoolRolloutPlan create/promote/abort for the above pools only.
- Routing supports stage partition selection for the above pools only.
- Gate evaluation should default to fail-closed on missing telemetry for automated gates; manual override/force-promote remains admin-only with audit events.

Approvals required before execution:
- Product (Dedicated): Lauren Bishop
- Platform Eng sponsor: Priya Natarajan
- SRE/oncall readiness: Rafael Mendes or Sean Gallagher
- Security/RBAC/audit review touchpoint: Kaitlyn Nguyen (confirm admin-only overrides + audit events are present)

Rollout instructions (proposed):
1) Confirm current prod deploy includes required PRs:
   - redwood PRs: pr-28458 (promote/abort + audit), pr-28503 (routing split), pr-28521 (stage tags), pr-28592 (force-promote/hold guardrails)
   - observability-pack PR: pr-631 (dashboards/alerts templates)
2) Enable ff.dedicated_rollouts.staged.enabled scoped to account_id + pool_id(s).
3) Enable ff.console.dedicated_rollouts.staged_ui scoped to account_id.
4) If enabling automated gates for beta: enable ff.dedicated_rollouts.gates_automated_v1 for the same scope.
5) Verify:
   - Console: rollout timeline renders; "Gate evidence" panel populates.
   - Metrics: stage_id labels present but within allowlist; no cardinality spikes.
   - Routing: stage partition does not starve capacity (watch 429s/queue depth on canary stage).
6) Post-enablement notify #support + #eng-oncall with the list of enabled accounts/pools and rollback steps.

Rollback instructions:
- Disable ff.console.dedicated_rollouts.staged_ui first (hides UI).
- Disable ff.dedicated_rollouts.gates_automated_v1 (if set).
- Disable ff.dedicated_rollouts.staged.enabled (control plane + routing path off).
- If a rollout is mid-stage: use abort action first when possible; if control plane is impaired, force-hold then revert flag.

Notes / risks:
- Known beta risk: canary partitioning can reduce effective capacity and cause unexpected 429s if pool is small or traffic is bursty. Ensure partitioning guidelines are followed and min headroom validation is on.
- Telemetry tag mismatch can cause gate evidence to appear empty; verify stage_tag schema after enablement.

References:
- Confluence runbook: runbook-dedicated-staged-rollouts-operations
- Audit event spec: audit-events-for-dedicated-rollout-actions
- Capacity guidelines: dedicated-pool-partitioning-guidelines-for-staged-rollouts

title: Telemetry query timeouts triggered by incident-bot bursts in #incidents (v2 context cards) causing slow/empty diagnostics
preview:
Issue Summary
During several customer-impacting events and internal incident drills, incident-bot v2 telemetry context cards intermittently rendered as empty or partially complete. Slack threads showed repeated “Telemetry query timed out” messages. Investigation indicates the bot can generate bursty query patterns (multiple responders + retries + interactive actions), which saturates the telemetry query service and triggers timeouts. These timeouts are most acute under incident conditions (elevated error rates + higher on-call activity), which is exactly when the bot is relied upon.

Impact
- On-call responders lost fast access to saturation signals (GPU util / queue depth / KV cache pressure), increasing time-to-triage.
- Telemetry query service experienced elevated p95/p99 latency; in worst cases, query workers hit concurrency limits and began failing fast.
- Secondary impact: dashboards and ad-hoc queries in the same time window degraded (shared query infrastructure).

Environment
- Production (primarily us-east, eu-west)
- Affects Slack channels: #incidents, #eng-oncall
- Affected service(s): telemetry-query-service (internal), incident-bot (Slack app)

Customer / Data sensitivity
Internal issue. No evidence of customer data exposure; failures were timeouts/empty cards only.

What we observed
- Bot posts initial incident context, then multiple responders click “Refresh signals” / “Expand details” within a short window.
- Slack interactive action retries (due to ack deadline + transient network latency) caused duplicate calls.
- Bot performed per-card, per-signal queries with limited coalescing; same incident/thread triggers repeated identical queries.

Symptoms (from bot / logs)
- Slack card banner: “Saturation signals unavailable (telemetry query timed out). Showing dashboard links.”
- telemetry-query-service logs: request_id spikes and increased “deadline exceeded” counts
- p99 latency spikes correlate with incident start + the first 10 minutes of heavy responder traffic

Steps to reproduce (reliable in staging with load)
1) Start a simulated incident thread and trigger bot context cards.
2) Have 5–10 users click “Refresh” and “Expand” within 60 seconds.
3) Observe telemetry query latency and bot timeouts, particularly if the telemetry cluster is already under moderate load.

Investigation notes
- Primary driver is burst concurrency and lack of request coalescing at the telemetry-query-service boundary.
- The bot also issues some high-cost queries (wide time windows + high-cardinality labels) when the incident metadata is incomplete (missing route/service scoping), causing fallback to broader queries.
- Slack retry behavior: interactive actions must ACK quickly; when upstream query exceeds budget, the bot may retry, amplifying bursts.

Mitigation / Fix
Implemented a multi-layer approach:
1) Cache + request coalescing for telemetry queries used by incident-bot
   - Short TTL caching (target 30–120s depending on signal class)
   - Per-(incident_id, region, service, route) de-duplication to collapse identical queries
   - Serve stale-on-error during incident windows (prefer last-known-good + links)

2) Rate limits + circuit breakers on diagnostics endpoints
   - Token bucket per bot identity + per incident thread
   - Hard cap on concurrent in-flight queries per region
   - Circuit breaker: when telemetry p95 exceeds threshold, bot switches to degraded mode automatically (links + cached summaries)

3) Tighter query budgets + bounded time windows
   - Default windows reduced for “first 5 minutes” cards
   - If incident metadata lacks scoping, bot displays an action to “Select service/region” instead of fanning out

4) Perf canary for telemetry query latency (bot-critical queries)
   - Synthetic queries run continuously
   - Alerts on sustained p95/p99 regression and timeout rate

Resolution
- Rolled out caching + timeout handling improvements in incident-bot.
- Added server-side rate limits/circuit breakers in diagnostics endpoints.
- Deployed telemetry query latency perf canary and alerting.
- Verified during two internal incident simulations and one production incident: bot returned cached summaries quickly and did not amplify telemetry load.

Verification
- Success criteria:
  - Bot context card render within 3s p95 (degraded mode allowed) in prod during load tests
  - Telemetry query timeout rate < 1% for bot-critical query set (sustained)
  - No >2x increase in telemetry-query-service QPS attributable to bot refresh bursts
- Observed (post-change):
  - Bot “saturation signals” card returns within ~1–2s when cached, ~2–4s uncached; falls back to links after budget expires
  - Telemetry query p99 no longer spikes linearly with Slack action bursts

Follow-ups / Action Items
- Document query budget and allowed label sets for incident-bot (owner: Allison Grant)
- Add guardrail test to prevent introducing high-cardinality dimensions into bot queries (owner: Nadia Rahman)
- Confirm bot’s Slack retries are idempotent across all interactive actions (owner: Devon Price)
- Update on-call troubleshooting playbook for empty cards to reflect degraded-mode behavior and where to check perf canary (owner: Rafael Mendes)

Comments
2025-02-18 (Miles Thornton): Creating ticket after multiple reports that incident-bot v2 saturation cards are timing out during bursts in #incidents. We need a fix before expanding beta beyond SRE rotation.

2025-02-18 (Logan Wright): Looking at telemetry-query-service. Seeing concurrency spikes + deadline exceeded clustered around bot activity. Suspect missing cache/coalescing. Will propose TTL caching and a per-thread rate limit.

2025-02-19 (Jacob Stein): From bot logs, Slack interactive actions retried when ack arrives late. We’re not fully idempotent on refresh path; duplicates are generating identical telemetry queries.

2025-02-20 (Nadia Rahman): Confirmed some queries are unbounded when route/service labels missing. Recommend forcing scoping in bot UI or using safe defaults with narrow windows.

2025-02-24 (Logan Wright): Implemented server-side throttles + circuit breaker config in diagnostics endpoints. Initial load test: QPS is capped; bot falls back to cached context + links.

2025-03-03 (Aisha Bello): Adding perf canary to catch regressions earlier; alert will page obs-oncall only (not #incidents) to avoid noise.

2025-03-06 (Miles Thornton): Verified during drill. Cards load reliably; when telemetry is slow, we get cached summary and deep links. Closing as Resolved.

  - Bot context card render within 3s p95 (degraded mode allowed) in prod during load tests
  - Telemetry query timeout rate < 1% for bot-critical query set (sustained)
  - No >2x increase in telemetry-query-service QPS attributable to bot refresh bursts
- Observed (post-change):
  - Bot “saturation signals” card returns within ~1–2s when cached, ~2–4s uncached; falls back to links after budget expires
  - Telemetry query p99 no longer spikes linearly with Slack action bursts

Follow-ups / Action Items
- Document query budget and allowed label sets for incident-bot (owner: Allison Grant)
- Add guardrail test to prevent introducing high-cardinality dimensions into bot queries (owner: Nadia Rahman)
- Confirm bot’s Slack retries are idempotent across all interactive actions (owner: Devon Price)
- Update on-call troubleshooting playbook for empty cards to reflect degraded-mode behavior and where to check perf canary (owner: Rafael Mendes)

Comments
2025-02-18 (Miles Thornton): Creating ticket after multiple reports that incident-bot v2 saturation cards are timing out during bursts in #incidents. We need a fix before expanding beta beyond SRE rotation.

2025-02-18 (Logan Wright): Looking at telemetry-query-service. Seeing concurrency spikes + deadline exceeded clustered around bot activity. Suspect missing cache/coalescing. Will propose TTL caching and a per-thread rate limit.

2025-02-19 (Jacob Stein): From bot logs, Slack interactive actions retried when ack arrives late. We’re not fully idempotent on refresh path; duplicates are generating identical telemetry queries.

2025-02-20 (Nadia Rahman): Confirmed some queries are unbounded when route/service labels missing. Recommend forcing scoping in bot UI or using safe defaults with narrow windows.

2025-02-24 (Logan Wright): Implemented server-side throttles + circuit breaker config in diagnostics endpoints. Initial load test: QPS is capped; bot falls back to cached context + links.

2025-03-03 (Aisha Bello): Adding perf canary to catch regressions earlier; alert will page obs-oncall only (not #incidents) to avoid noise.

2025-03-06 (Miles Thornton): Verified during drill. Cards load reliably; when telemetry is slow, we get cached summary and deep links. Closing as Resolved.

title: Add SDK guardrail: detect 'Bearer ' prefix in api_key and warn across SDKs
preview: Background: Support saw multiple customer-reported auth issues due to key/header formatting across SDKs (SUP-4821, SUP-4884, SUP-4902, SUP-4933). Also update docs and support playbook.

title: Provision dedicated GPU benchmark runner capacity for Runtime LTS perf gates (avoid blocking CI)
preview:
Request
- We need dedicated GPU runner capacity for perf-canary benchmarks used as *release gates* for Runtime LTS patch releases.
- Current state: perf jobs are contending with general CI and ad-hoc benchmark runs, causing queue times and occasional partial coverage during patch week.

Why this matters
- LTS patches are blocked on perf gate results (p50/p95 latency + throughput across canonical workloads). Any runner starvation delays releases and increases risk of releasing without full coverage.
- We also need to avoid impacting regular developer CI and nightly runs.

Scope
- Provision/allocate a separate runner pool (or reserved quota) for perf-canary “release-branch / LTS gate” workflows.
- Ensure capacity exists for concurrent runs during patch week (RC validation + reruns for flakes) without starving mainline.

Requirements (from Runtime LTS workstream)
- GPU SKUs: H100 (primary), A100 (secondary) at minimum.
- Concurrency target: 6 parallel benchmark jobs (H100) + 2 parallel (A100) during patch week; outside patch week, allow scale down to 2+1.
- Runtime: each job ~45–70 minutes wall time; expect 2–3 full gate cycles per RC (initial run + rerun(s)).
- Determinism: pin driver/container image; avoid noisy neighbors (dedicated nodes preferred).

Acceptance criteria
- New runner pool available to perf-canary with labels (e.g., runner_group=lts-perf-gate).
- perf-canary workflows updated to target that pool for release/* and runtime-*-lts branches.
- Documented scheduling policy + escalation path if capacity is exhausted.

Owners / stakeholders
- Requestor: Sahana Iyer (CI/build)
- Release Eng: Connor O’Brien
- Runtime perf: Marcus Reed / Jonas Weber
- SRE: Sean Gallagher (assignee), Rafael Mendes (backup)

References
- Slack: #eng-sre / “lts-perf-gate-thresholds” and #eng-infra / runner capacity discussion
- Related: INT-3302 for release-branch CI permissions/secrets; ENG-4825 for perf gate integration into LTS release.

title: Enable Hosted data residency enforcement feature flag in staging + seed test orgs/API keys for verification
preview:
Background
We are preparing end-to-end staging verification for Hosted data residency enforcement (region pinning + deterministic blocks + standardized errors). This internal support request is to enable the staging feature flags and seed a few test orgs/projects/API keys so Engineering + SRE + Security can validate routing, fallback behavior, logging boundaries, and the public error contract.

Request
1) Enable residency enforcement feature flags in staging
- Enable residency policy evaluation + enforcement in gateway + routing.
- Ensure cross-region fallback is disabled by default when residency constraints are present.

2) Seed staging test entities
Create the following in staging control plane (do not use real customer data):
A) Org: "residency-e2e-eu-only" (owner: platform-test@redwood.example)
- Project: "hosted-chat"
- Residency policy: allowed_regions=[EU], primary_region=EU (EU region group as defined in internal mapping)
- API key(s):
  - Key 1: project-scoped (standard)
  - Key 2: key-level override (optional) to validate precedence rules

B) Org: "residency-e2e-us-only"
- Project: "hosted-chat"
- Residency policy: allowed_regions=[US], primary_region=US
- API key: project-scoped

C) Org: "residency-e2e-unpinned"
- Project: "hosted-chat"
- No residency policy (control behavior baseline)

Notes / constraints
- Do not paste full API keys into this ticket. Store in 1Password shared vault "Staging Test Credentials" under item: "INT-9312 residency e2e keys".
- Ensure audit log emission is enabled for policy create/update/delete in staging (for evidence + UI troubleshooting).
- Ensure request logs for blocked requests include residency decision context fields (policy_id, evaluated_allowed_regions, selected_region, block_reason, request_id) without logging prompt/content.

Approvals needed
- Security Engineering sign-off for staging enablement: Marcus Lin
- Product owner sign-off for staging test plan: Naomi Feldman

Success criteria
- Staging environment can deterministically reproduce a residency block and returns the standardized error contract for both streaming and non-streaming endpoints.
- Routing does not attempt cross-region fallback when policy prohibits it.
- Console shows the seeded policies and recent blocked requests, and audit log shows config changes.

References
- Slack: #eng-sre (thread on alerting + enablement) and #eng-platform (schema/policy precedence)
- Planned enforcement/error contract PRs: PR-21507, PR-21533
- Related support bug being validated in staging: SUP-28577 (streaming returned generic 500 previously)

title: Break-glass access request audit — time-bound elevation for Private control plane role binding fix (Northbridge POC)
preview:
Request type: Break-glass (emergency admin elevation)

Requester: Colin O'Donnell (Solutions Eng, Private customer pilot lead)
Requested by (ticket reporter): Fiona McCarthy (SecOps)
System(s): Redwood Private Control Plane (customer VPC), RBAC v2 enabled (pilot)
Customer context: Northbridge Healthcare (Private POC)

Reason / business justification:
- During a scheduled POC validation window, Northbridge admins were blocked from exporting audit logs and rotating an API key due to a mis-scoped role binding created during initial bootstrap.
- Customer impact: POC validation blocked; risk of missing security sign-off milestone.
- Standard path (customer admin fixes via Console) not possible because the affected binding controlled Console admin access.

Scope of requested access:
- Minimal actions required:
  1) Read RBAC bindings for org "northbridge-healthcare" in the private control plane
  2) Add a single temporary role binding granting "Read-only Auditor" and "Security Admin" to the customer-admin IdP group (SAML group mapping)
  3) Verify audit log export endpoint returns 200 and that authorization decisions are being logged
- Explicitly out of scope:
  - No model data access
  - No prompt/content inspection
  - No changes to routing policies, deployment config, or retention settings

Requested duration:
- 90 minutes time-bound elevation
- Elevation window requested: 2025-02-11 18:30–20:00 PT

Approvals (per break-glass policy):
- Approver #1 (Security Eng): Marcus Lin — APPROVED 2025-02-11 18:22 PT
- Approver #2 (SecOps/on-call): Fiona McCarthy — APPROVED 2025-02-11 18:24 PT
- FYI/exec visibility (not required but notified due to regulated customer): Dr. Aisha Rahman — ACK 2025-02-11 18:26 PT

Access method:
- Break-glass role: "redwood.breakglass.admin" (time-bound)
- Mechanism: Just-in-time elevation via internal admin service (BGW), scoped to org_id=org_7Qp1N (Northbridge Healthcare)
- MFA: required and verified

Execution notes (what was done):
- Verified current bindings: customer-admin IdP group mapped to an outdated role template missing "audit.export" permission.
- Added corrected role bindings at org scope only.
- Confirmed that bindings propagate and authz cache invalidation fired (policy evaluator cache flush for org scope).

Verification / validation:
- Customer re-tested "Export audit logs" in Console: success
- Customer re-tested API key rotation endpoint: success
- Confirmed audit log events include authz decision context fields (decision=allow) for both actions.

Access revocation:
- Break-glass role auto-expired.
- Confirmed no lingering elevated session tokens.

Evidence artifacts captured:
- Change record: control-plane audit event IDs (see below)
- Approval trail: recorded in this ticket and BGW request ID
- Post-event review: documented under Comments

Sensitive identifiers (for internal use only):
- BGW request ID: BGW-2025-02-11-1830-PT-0427
- Target org_id: org_7Qp1N (Northbridge Healthcare)
- Target private cluster: nbhc-private-us-west-1
- Audit event IDs:
  - evt_aud_01JMB3H0KZ2R9S6R (binding.read)
  - evt_aud_01JMB3N4F1QX2D8W (binding.create)
  - evt_aud_01JMB3Q9Q0G8C3VJ (audit.export.verify)

- Slack: #eng-security thread "break-glass procedure and alerting" (ref: sources/slack/eng-security/1739635544-break-glass-procedure-and-alerting.json)
- Slack: #support "customer seeing permission denied after role migration" (ref: sources/slack/support/1740065522-customer-seeing-permission-denied-after-role-migration.json)

title: Staging: Private control-plane backup CronJob failing with RBAC "forbidden" (cannot list/get secrets + Redwood CRs)
preview:
Issue summary
- The scheduled backup CronJob deployed via Helm in staging is failing immediately due to missing RBAC permissions.
- This blocks our nightly backup verification and is causing noise for on-call.

Impact
- Staging backup schedule is effectively disabled (no successful backups since enabling the chart values).
- No customer impact (staging only), but this blocks Day-2 ops hardening and restore drill automation.

Environment
- Cluster: private-staging-us-east-1
- Namespace: redwood-private
- Chart: redwood-private (backup CronJob enabled)
- Backup storage backend: S3 (staging bucket)

Symptoms
- CronJob pods crash with "forbidden" errors when attempting to enumerate resources required for control-plane backup.

Steps to reproduce
1) Deploy/upgrade Helm release with backup enabled:
   - values.backup.enabled=true
   - values.backup.schedule="0 2 * * *"
2) Wait for CronJob to create a Job.
3) Inspect logs for the Job pod:
   kubectl -n redwood-private logs job/<job-name>

Observed logs (redacted)
- E0122 02:01:14.123] error: failed preflight: unable to read cluster state: secrets is forbidden: User "system:serviceaccount:redwood-private:redwood-backup" cannot list resource "secrets" in API group "" in the namespace "redwood-private"
- E0122 02:01:14.124] error: failed inventory: redwoodtenants.platform.redwood.ai is forbidden: User "system:serviceaccount:redwood-private:redwood-backup" cannot list resource "redwoodtenants" in API group "platform.redwood.ai" in the namespace "redwood-private"

Expected behavior
- Backup CronJob service account should have the minimal RBAC required to read backup inputs (config/state CRs, configmaps, select secrets or secret references) and create the backup artifact.

Notes
- This appears to be a Helm chart RBAC template gap (service account is created, but Role/RoleBinding is incomplete/missing required verbs/resources when backup is enabled).

title: Wi-Fi EAP-TLS certificate rotation fails after imaging, blocking SCCM/Intune policy application
preview: Issue summary: After new-image provisioning via SCCM/Autopilot + Intune, fleet devices intermittently fail to connect to corporate Wi-Fi. Impact: users cannot reach internal services until manual remediation; affects small percentage of newly imaged devices in US-east. Root cause suspected during certificate rotation flow: machine certificate is not present in the local machine store at time Wi‑Fi profile with EAP-TLS is applied, causing profile application to fail and network location services to stall.
